name: Tochigi Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the abundance of nature in Tochigi
purity and the snows of the Nikko highlands

description: A green field charged at the centre with a white emblem made of three nested peaked-roof shapes above a small horizontal element, stylising the katakana 'to' (ト) for Tochigi.
history: Tochigi's prefectural symbol was selected by public competition and adopted on 1 July 1962, with the flag (the symbol on a green field) in use from the same date.

features.symbolism: the verdant fields of the Kanto plain and the forests of the Nikko mountains

features.role: stylised katakana 'to' (ト) for 'Tochigi', drawn as three concentric peak-and-valley rooftops above a horizontal base
features.symbolism: the katakana 'to' for Tochigi, rendered as a peaked form evoking the mountains of Nikko, with concentric ridges symbolising the prefecture's harmony, growth, and forward motion.
